name: Mallos de Riglos & Mirador de los Buitres — return via the Sierra de Loarre
name_original: Mallos de Riglos & Mirador de los Buitres — vuelta por la Sierra de Loarre
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Ayerbe traverse the diverse landscapes of the Aragonese Pre-Pyrenees, situated in the Hoya de Huesca comarca. The region is characterized by the dramatic rock formations of the Kingdom of the Mallos, including the Mallos de Riglos and Mallos de Agüero. Routes often follow the Gállego River Valley, offering less strenuous sections, and ascend into the Pre-Pyrenean mountains of Loarre and Luna. The terrain features a mix of pine, olive, and almond trees, with generally dry, loose, and stony soil. Best touring cycling routes around Ayerbe The most popular touring cycling route is Mallos de Riglos & Mirador de los Buitres — return via the Sierra de Loarre, a 25.5 miles (41.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Mallos de Riglos and the Vultures' Viewpoint. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vultures Viewpoint – Loarre Castle loop from Ayerbe, a difficult 23.6 miles (37.9 km) path. This route provides access to the Mirador de los Buitres and the historic Loarre Castle. Local touring cyclists also love the Peñas de Riglos – Mallos de Riglos loop from Ayerbe, a 25.6 miles (41.2 km) trail leading through the impressive Mallos de Riglos formations, often completed in about 3 hours 59 minutes. Touring cycling around Ayerbe is defined by the towering Mallos rock formations, the scenic Gállego River Valley, and the varied Pre-Pyrenean mountain terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, including challenging ascents. The routes in Ayerbe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ayerbe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Ayerbe?
A: The terrain around Ayerbe is highly varied, reflecting its location in the Aragonese Pre-Pyrenees. You'll encounter dramatic rock formations like the Mallos de Riglos and Mallos de Agüero, sections along the Gállego River Valley with minimal altitude gain, and ascents into the Pre-Pyrenean mountains of Loarre and Luna. The landscape features a mix of pine, olive, and almond trees, with generally dry, loose, and stony soil.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in the Ayerbe area?
A: While many routes in Ayerbe involve significant elevation, the &lt;p&gt;Gállego River Valley offers particularly appealing features for touring cyclists, especially those with families. It provides paved roads with minimal altitude gain, running parallel to the river, allowing for enjoyable, less strenuous rides. You can even stop for a dip in the river's pools during summer.&lt;/p&gt;

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see while cycling around Ayerbe?
A: Ayerbe's routes are rich with sights. You can cycle past the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas de Riglos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Agüero&lt;/a&gt; rock formations. Many routes also offer views of the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3983714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loarre Castle&lt;/a&gt;, one of Europe's best-preserved Romanesque castles, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/793375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marcuello Castle&lt;/a&gt; with its breathtaking views. The Mirador de los Buitres (Vultures' Viewpoint) is another highlight, offering opportunities for birdwatching.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Ayerbe?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, but early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable. Winters are generally mild but can have cooler temperatures, especially at higher elevations.

Q: How difficult are the touring cycling routes around Ayerbe?
A: The routes around Ayerbe cater to a range of abilities. While there are options with minimal altitude gain along the Gállego River, many routes involve challenging ascents into the Pre-Pyrenean mountains. Out of the 36 touring cycling routes available, 13 are rated as moderate and 23 are rated as difficult, indicating a prevalence of more challenging rides.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Ayerbe?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vultures Viewpoint – Loarre Castle loop from Ayerbe&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 23.6-mile (37.9 km) path that takes you to both the Mirador de los Buitres and Loarre Castle. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38155620" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas de Riglos – Mallos de Riglos loop from Ayerbe&lt;/a&gt;, a 25.6-mile (41.2 km) trail through the impressive Mallos de Riglos formations.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Ayerbe?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ayerbe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the dramatic scenery, particularly the towering Mallos rock formations, the varied Pre-Pyrenean mountain terrain, and the sense of adventure offered by the challenging ascents.

Q: Can I find routes that combine natural beauty with historical sites?
A: Absolutely. The region is perfect for combining natural wonders with historical exploration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975143941/mallos-de-riglos-mirador-de-los-buitres-return-via-the-sierra-de-loarre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Riglos & Mirador de los Buitres — return via the Sierra de Loarre&lt;/a&gt; route offers views of the Mallos de Riglos and the Vultures' Viewpoint, while also traversing the Sierra de Loarre, which is home to the historic Loarre Castle.

Q: Are there longer touring routes available for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking longer distances and significant elevation, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Piedras de Agüero – Mallos de Agüero loop from Riglos-Concilio&lt;/a&gt; offer a challenging 37.8-mile (60.8 km) ride with over 1600 meters of elevation gain, taking you through the impressive Mallos de Agüero.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for touring cycling routes around Ayerbe?
A: Route distances vary significantly. You can find routes ranging from around 19 miles (30 km), such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31502436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Riglos – Riglos loop from Ayerbe&lt;/a&gt;, which takes about 2 hours, to longer, more challenging rides like the 37.8-mile (60.8 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Piedras de Agüero – Mallos de Agüero loop from Riglos-Concilio&lt;/a&gt;, which can take over 6 hours to complete.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach Ayerbe with a bicycle?
A: Ayerbe is situated in the Hoya de Huesca comarca of Aragon. While specific public transport options for bikes can vary, regional bus services often connect smaller towns. It's advisable to check with local transport providers in advance regarding their policies for carrying bicycles, especially for longer journeys.

Q: Are there any services for cyclists in Ayerbe, such as bike rentals or repair shops?
A: As a hub for outdoor activities, Ayerbe and nearby larger towns in the Hoya de Huesca comarca may offer services catering to cyclists. For specific bike rentals or repair shops, it's recommended to inquire locally upon arrival or check online resources for businesses in Ayerbe or the closest major town, Huesca.
